introSection: Giant Coaster was a steel roller coaster located at Fuji-Q Highland in Fujiyoshida, Yamanashi, Japan. It is believed to have been the longest roller coaster in the world when it opened. A car from the Giant Coaster still exists and was on display at Fuji-Q in 2019.

sections.title: Trains
sections.content: 5 cars per train. Riders are arranged 2 across in 3 rows for a total of 30 riders per train.

fullText:
Giant Coaster was a steel roller coaster located at Fuji-Q Highland in Fujiyoshida, Yamanashi, Japan. It is believed to have been the longest roller coaster in the world when it opened. A car from the Giant Coaster still exists and was on display at Fuji-Q in 2019.



5 cars per train. Riders are arranged 2 across in 3 rows for a total of 30 riders per train.
